title: haplopappus inuloides var. howellii
intro: Haplopappus inuloides var. howellii, also known as Howell's false goldenaster, is a plant species in the sunflower family that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing swelling. The plant's health benefits include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and act as an antioxidant, reducing the risk of chronic diseases. Herbal preparations of Haplopappus inuloides var. howellii can be found in tea, tincture, and salve form, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
